name: Kaipapa'u Gulch Trail
tagline: A quiet windward gulch hike through guava and kukui forest — you'll likely have the whole trail to yourself.
parking: Free roadside parking along Hau'ula Homestead Road near the trailhead — very limited
bestTime: Morning for the best light filtering through the forest canopy
talkStory: About 4 miles round trip with 600 ft elevation gain through a lush gulch in the windward Ko'olaus. The trail parallels a stream and passes through stands of kukui nut, guava, and hau trees. It's not a destination hike — no big waterfall or summit — but the solitude and forest ambiance are the whole point. Trail can be overgrown in sections, so long pants are a good call. Combine with nearby Hau'ula Loop for a longer day. Mosquitoes are aggressive — repellent is non-negotiable.
